title: The Sentience of the Great Yui sama
display_title: The Sentience of the Great Yui sama
wikitext:
[]: The Sentience of the Great Yui-sama             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Nov 12, 2013 11:57 am There's a tangential bit about this going on in an Asuka thread, so I figured I might as well make one for it here discussing the subject in a more general manner. The tangent in question is discussing whether or not Yui was 'awake' inside of Unit-01 for the duration of the show, select periods of time, or only under exact stimuli that are hard to create. The only evidence that I see for 100% sentience (that Reichu mentioned, IIRC) is that Gendo seems to spend time talking directly to Unit-01's head. Whatever we come up with will probably wind up changing a lot of the way that we think about Evangelions, so discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 12, 2013 1:24 pm I'm mostly confused by the arguments that there wouldn't be a basic level of awareness, but then my basic assumptions going in seem to be quite different from the norm. My take: - Evas are basically giant (alien) humans who are restrained and controlled through the use of technology that violates the central nervous system and core. - Evas aren't born with souls and, in order to be useful in any way, must receive one from a Lilin donor. - As we see with Rei and Kaworu, transferring a soul from one body to another results in that soul having ownership of a new body, and, through the symbiotic soul-body link, the two influence each other. The soul influences who someone is and how they behave, much as the vessel actively reshapes the soul. - Going by NGE's own precepts, it is entirely reasonable to say that Yui's new body is Eva-01. She is the Eva, and the Eva is her. Hence if there is any "primordial consciousness" in the picture, it is entirely her own. - The exact means by which Nerv wrests bodily control away from the Evas, and how the Evas get it back in turn, is poorly understood and seems to vary wildly depending on the needs of the plot. However, one point that Anno was quite emphatic about is that the Evas are barely under humanity's control . -- Taking into account this notion that complete control is basically impossible, it's reasonable to surmise that the Evas, despite being immobile most of the time, are quite sentient. In other words, brain function and basic sensory operations along with 'spiritual awareness' cannot be fully suppressed. --- Indeed, Ritsuko (I think) insinuates as much when she wonders if the Evas hate their keepers, in episode 16. Maybe that makes a modicum of sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Tue Nov 12, 2013 1:48 pm Suddenly, the Evas are a lot more intimidating beings with their consciousness and awareness of their surroundings. I'll now never be able to look at the scene with Asuka yelling at Kyoko, calling her puppet, the same without wondering if that was the straw that broke the camel's back into their synch ratio (and relationship in general), or if she hurt her "feelings" with that tirade of hers. NGE seems to have a very broad definition humanity, I'm just realizing this now and that I may to need to re-watch NGE to study the Evas and their actions a bit more.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Nov 12, 2013 1:58 pm It makes perfect sense, which also means that I now have to back now and think, "What did Yui think of this?" or "What did Kyoko think of that?"

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Tue Nov 12, 2013 3:27 pm I have a few questions of my own: Is this the real reason Gendo picked Shinji to pilot the Eva? Because Yui's soul was in unit 01 and he knew they would a higher synchronization than what Rei could achieve? Also can I assume this is why unit 01 went berserk in episode 1? A part of Yui's consciousness was trying to save Shinji from being killed by the Angel? Or am I totaly wrong here..

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Nov 12, 2013 4:04 pm I think that's the general consensus, that Yui was going "RWAR GET AWAY FROM MY BABY BOI". As to Gendo's motives, it's pretty fanwanky.

[]:             Quote Post by Noah » Tue Nov 12, 2013 4:22 pm Dataprime wrote: I have a few questions of my own: Is this the real reason Gendo picked Shinji to pilot the Eva? Because Yui's soul was in unit 01 and he knew they would a higher synchronization than what Rei could achieve? Also can I assume this is why unit 01 went berserk in episode 1? A part of Yui's consciousness was trying to save Shinji from being killed by the Angel? Or am I totaly wrong here..

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Nov 12, 2013 5:14 pm Rei became a fifth dimensional consciousness. Yui and everyone else did not. Yui is entombed in the Eva, and is immortal in that sense, but she's neither omnipresent nor omniscient. The figures of Rei -the one that appears at the beginning, the ones that appear to collect the dead, and the one that appears to Shinji at the end- are all manifestations of the combined Lilith-Adam/Rei-Kaworu Entity. Rei appears to be its default form. As to Yui's consciousness: Gendo talks to the Eva, and addresses Yui. People also talk to the graves of their loved ones. The connection in this case is clear. There is simply no reason to assume that Yui (and by extension Kyoko and whoever or whatever is in Unit Zero) are sitting there staring into space and not moving the entire time. Especially since they require pilots to direct them and only under special circumstances do they move on their own, and when they do they don't act human at all. If the resident souls are fully conscious and the Eva is basically their body, why do they need pilots at all? The fact that Rei and Kaworu control their bodies doesn't imply a corresponding function in the Evas. Firstly, Rei and Kaworu's souls aren't merely souls, they're gods . Kaworu can control an Eva without even being inside it because it's an extension of himself. Yui (and Kyoko) are just people. There's no reason to assume they would have the same vast metaphysical powers (which is why, even though they're in Evas, they require an outside power source to generate an AT-Field that has any utility) The key to the function of the Evas is the parent-child bond. They can move on their own when the soul's child is in danger. An engine that never shuts off doesn't need a key. Besides all that, the Evas are living things that are clearly hateful, violent monsters. The souls animate them, and the mother-child bond can control them, or if the child is in danger, allow the mother to more fully control them. Hence Unit Zero's rampage. It wasn't lashing out at anyone in particular, it was lashing out and finally ended up trying to kill itself, hence bashing its own head into the wall over and over. There's also the issue of the power source. The Evas need power to move. Without it, they can't even stand up (unless it looks cool). That alone suggests that when they're not switched on, they're basically asleep.

[]:             Quote Post by Noah » Tue Nov 12, 2013 8:00 pm Chuckman wrote: There is simply no reason to assume that Yui (and by extension Kyoko and whoever or whatever is in Unit Zero) are sitting there staring into space and not moving the entire time. Especially since they require pilots to direct them and only under special circumstances do they move on their own, and when they do they don't act human at all.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Nov 12, 2013 8:04 pm Chuckman wrote: Yui (and Kyoko) are just people. There's no reason to assume they would have the same vast metaphysical powers (which is why, even though they're in Evas, they require an outside power source to generate an AT-Field that has any utility)

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Nov 12, 2013 8:10 pm Obviously, when Yui is awake and freaking out because Shinji is in danger, the Eva can move without external power. The rest of the time, it freezes or goes limp without batteries or current from the umbilical. Note also that the arm movement to deflect the lights in Ep 1 is followed by the arm going frozen afterwards, implying great effort was involved in moving it. That limitation isn't there in EoE when she bursts out of the bakelite, incidentally, so she was either unconscious or chose not to move. At that point she had an S2 engine, which is both philosopher's stone and Krell machine. The berserk Eva moving while completely exhausted may partly explain the savagery. She's cranky 'cause she's tired.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Nov 12, 2013 8:20 pm A rule that only applies until it doesn't isn't actually a rule. Clearly the evas ARE to some extent awake and aware or else Yui would never have known Shinji needed any saving in ep1, and considering some of the most impressive beatdowns Yui delivers all happen when the power is out suggests that the only thing that actually needs eternal power is the control system keeping the eva in check.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Nov 12, 2013 8:28 pm Would you have to be awake to react to your child's voice? If one is asleep, one can also wake up. If the Evas only need power to stop them from moving why aren't they freaking out all the time, especially the one that got pissed off and tried to destroy the base? Again, we come back around to: If they're conscious and able to move the whole time, then they spend most of their existence just standing there doing nothing . Okay, maybe Yui has the mental fortitude of a monk to keep totally frozen and not betray her plans, but why would Kyoko who didn't sign up for this shit (and may be crazy) do that, too? If it keeps the Evas in check, why don't they flip out when the lose power, which happens repeatedly during the series? The Evas can't move without power. Shinji's can because he's the Chosen One and he can unlock the fabulous secret powers of his empathic weapon when no one else can. Also, his Eva is the only one that's a clone of Lilith , who may not have or need a core.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Nov 12, 2013 8:51 pm Chuckman wrote: If the Evas only need power to stop them from moving why aren't they freaking out all the time, especially the one that got pissed off and tried to destroy the base?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Nov 12, 2013 9:07 pm How are they full on if the have no power? I'm not talking about standing around in the cages, I'm talking about when they run out of power completely and have no cables attached, like after Shamshel or Israfel when they run out of power and just stop moving . The controls being full on all the time makes no sense if they're not hooked up to external power and their batteries are empty. The system needing power to keep them going on a rampage or even moving on their own and doing whatever is completely contrary to multiple instances of multiple Evas having no power and not moving at all. Unit One goes berserk three times. One of those three, the power cable is still connected. One time, the battery has been depleted after hours of running in a low power mode. The third time, it ran out of power after depleting the batteries quickly. In all three, it eventually just stopped moving on its own. Berserk movement doesn't seem to have much to do with whether it has electricity from a battery or the cable at all. It's also a major point during the berserker sequences that no one expects that to happen. The assumed result by all involved is that no power = no movement (except Gendo and Fuyutsuki, who have inside information). There's a difference between assuming that Ritsuko doesn't have total information and assuming that she's a moron who doesn't grasp the basic functions of the system. When they want to stop Unit Zero from moving, they cut the power and wait for the battery to run out . Why would they cut the power if they need the power to control it? It's more likely that the rules understood to apply to the standard issue Eva don't apply to Shinji and his Eva because he's the protagonist than it is that the way Evas work is completely backwards from everything that's ever stated or implied on the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 12, 2013 9:29 pm Chuckman wrote: There is simply no reason to assume that Yui (and by extension Kyoko and whoever or whatever is in Unit Zero) are sitting there staring into space and not moving the entire time. Especially since they require pilots to direct them and only under special circumstances do they move on their own, and when they do they don't act human at all. If the resident souls are fully conscious and the Eva is basically their body, why do they need pilots at all?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Nov 12, 2013 9:37 pm Then why is one of them acquiring a power source a plot point?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 12, 2013 9:48 pm Chuckman wrote: Then why is one of them acquiring a power source a plot point?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Nov 12, 2013 9:54 pm My whole argument rests on them being immobilzed/asleep when they have no power supply. Unless they get really mad. Edit: It kinda rests on that. The argument against them just standing there all the time fully awake part rests on that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 12, 2013 10:13 pm Your language remains rather obfuscatory to me. If you're saying that Evas can't be sentient because they're normally "unplugged", welllll... Yes, normally they do need an external power source to provide energy to their muscles so they can run and punch and do somersaults and other energy-intensive things. However, as living creatures, Evas are certainly capable of storing energy in their bodies for short-term use. Add onto this the fact that Nerv performs regular biological upkeep on the Evas -- in other words, they're not just sitting around being biologically inert all the time. There's really no reason why they can't be suffering from a kind of "locked-in syndrome". Moreover, as I indicated earlier, the Evas' sentience is implied quite directly. Ritsuko wouldn't have wondered if the Evas "hate us" if they couldn't perceive the way they were being treated, and Ritsuko knows better than most.
